boundary.declared: Inject per-invocation tool telemetry (latency, outcome, metrics) into the shared tool execution pipeline in tool/tool.ts:wrap(), writing structured JSONL records alongside existing session analytics. Zero changes to individual tool files.
boundary.consumer_purpose: Enable operators and analytics tools to query per-invocation tool performance (latency distributions, error rates, cache effectiveness) without modifying any of the ~80 individual tool implementations.

tool_count.source: smart_grep: Tool.define( across packages/opencode/src/tool/*.ts — 80 unique matches

session_analytics_jsonl.pattern: Each smart tool writes independently — no shared helper. All use AppFileSystem.ensureDir + appendLine.

tool_cache_otel.stats_api: cache.ts:206-217 — stats(): Effect<{size, hits, misses, evictions, estimatedBytes}>

dead_code.imports: Bus, CoordEvents — publishes SubagentPhaseChanged to Bus

no_artifact_log.result: ZERO matches. artifactLog does not exist in this codebase.

layers: Layer 1 (core): decode args → execute tool → truncate output (lines 178-199)
tool_execution_pipeline.pipeline_composition: Inside-out: core → retry → timeout → semaphore → span

existing_logging_patterns.tools_with_zero_logging: ~77 of 80 tools have NO Log.create, NO log.info, NO log.warn, NO log.error
existing_logging_patterns.cache_annotations: cache.ts has 6 Effect.annotateCurrentSpan calls — only file doing this
existing_logging_patterns.smart_tool_pattern: 6 smart tools write ad-hoc JSONL analytics — no shared helper, identical boilerplate duplicated 6 times

non_blocking.note: New tool test, possibly relevant but not blocking

pre_existing_debt.summary: Two tools in registry are placeholders (delegate.ts, session-diff.ts) — erodes trust in tool registry
ToolCache.getOrCompute is defined but never called — dead API surface
ToolCache.inflight map mutated via direct reference mutation bypassing Ref.update

metrics_schema.format: JSONL (one JSON object per line, append-only)

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of tool execution in milliseconds

success.description: true if tool completed without error

error_kind.description: One of: InvalidArgumentsError, ToolError, TimeoutError, TransientError, ValidationError, or null on success

output_bytes.description: Length of output string in bytes (post-truncation)

args_fields.description: Number of top-level fields in the decoded args object

cache_hit.description: Whether result came from cache (null if tool is not cacheable)

retry_attempt.description: Retry attempt number (0 = first attempt, omitted if no retries)

timed_out.description: Whether the tool hit the timeout (false if no timeout configured)

metrics_schema.privacy_note: No args values, no output content — only metrics and structural shapes. SHA256 hashes of content-derived values per usage-data-doctrine.

implementation_plan.strategy: Single-choke-point injection in tool/tool.ts wrap() function. Zero changes to individual tool files.

changes.change: Add import: import { AppFileSystem } from "@opencode-ai/core/filesystem" and import { InstanceState } from "@/effect/instance-state"
changes.reason: Need filesystem access to write JSONL, and instance state for session directory path

changes.change: Add fs: AppFileSystem.Service and instance: InstanceState access to the outer Effect.gen
changes.reason: Need these services to write telemetry

changes.change: Add Effect.tapBoth around core to capture timing, success/failure, and write JSONL record
changes.reason: tapBoth lets us observe both success and failure channels without altering the pipeline

changes.change: Add additional span attributes: tool.latency_ms, tool.success, tool.error_kind, tool.output_bytes
changes.reason: Enrich existing OTel spans with telemetry data

files_to_modify.approach: Wrap the core pipeline with Effect.tapBoth to emit telemetry on both success and error paths. Use Date.now() for wall-clock timing (matching existing smart tool pattern). Write to same analytics directory as existing smart_tool_usage.v1.jsonl.

changes.change: Export CacheStats interface (already exported at line 21, verify no changes needed)
changes.reason: Telemetry may need to read cache stats — but stats() API already exists

files_to_modify.approach: Minimal — cache already has stats() and OTel annotations. No changes needed if we read span attributes. Only add an export if the telemetry helper needs direct access.

implementation_plan.files_to_touch_zero: All ~78 individual tool files — NO changes needed

approach_detail.injection_point: tool/tool.ts:wrap() — inside the execute closure (line ~167), after `core` is defined, before pipeline composition
approach_detail.timing_method: Date.now() — matches existing pattern in smart-grep.ts:56, smart-bash.ts:76
approach_detail.telemetry_write: Effect.tapBoth — left (error) channel records error_kind + failure, right (success) channel records metrics. Both write to JSONL.
approach_detail.path_construction: Use InstanceState.context for instance.directory, construct path: {instance.directory}/docs/json/opencode/sessions/{sessionID}/analytics/tool_invocations.v1.jsonl
approach_detail.error_detection: Match on error class names: InvalidArgumentsError → 'InvalidArgumentsError', ToolError → 'ToolError', TimeoutError → 'TimeoutError', TransientError → 'TransientError', ValidationError → 'ValidationError', other → 'UnknownError'
approach_detail.cache_hit_detection: Read from current span via Effect.currentSpan and check span.attributes['cache.hit'] — or, simpler: check if ctx has a persistent cache-state reference. Alternative: check if toolInfo.cacheable is set and query ToolCache.stats() before/after to detect hit increment.
approach_detail.output_bytes: result.output.length (or JSON.stringify(result.output).length if not already a string)
approach_detail.retry_detection: NOT feasible in wrap() — retry happens via Effect.retry which re-executes the entire pipeline. Each attempt sees only its own execution. Alternative: omit from v1, or add via Effect.tap on the retry layer wrapper.

options.name: span_attribute_read
options.description: Read cache.hit span attribute after execution. The cache sets 'cache.hit': true on line 104. Use Effect.currentSpan to read it.
options.feasibility: MEDIUM — requires Effect.currentSpan which returns Option<Span>. Span may not be available in all contexts.
options.recommendation: FALLBACK — use if direct approach fails

options.name: stats_delta
options.description: Query ToolCache.stats() before and after execution. If hits increased by 1, it was a cache hit.
options.feasibility: LOW — requires ToolCache dependency injection in wrap(), adds overhead

options.name: tool_config_check
options.description: If toolInfo.cacheable is undefined → cache_hit: null. If toolInfo.cacheable is set but getOrCompute is deprecated → skip for v1, annotate cache_hit: null and add a TODO.
options.feasibility: HIGH — toolInfo.cacheable is already accessible in wrap()
options.recommendation: USE — set cache_hit: null for v1, with a TODO comment for future wiring. The cache's OTel span annotations already provide this data for OTel consumers. The JSONL record gets cache_hit: null as a placeholder until getOrCompute is re-wired.

cache_hit_strategy.decision: For v1, emit cache_hit: null for all tools. Cache hit/miss data is already captured in OTel spans (cache.ts:104-135). The JSONL telemetry record documents that the tool IS cacheable (cache_hit: null means 'cacheable but hit status not captured in JSONL — use OTel span'). If toolInfo.cacheable is undefined, emit cache_hit: null with cacheable: false metadata.

alternatives_considered.name: Per-tool Effect.tap injection
alternatives_considered.description: Add telemetry to each tool's execute function individually
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: 80 tool files to modify. Duplicated code. Violates DRY. Same telemetry data available at choke point.

alternatives_considered.name: Plugin hook (tool.execute.after)
alternatives_considered.description: Use existing plugin system at session/tools.ts:118
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Plugin hooks fire AFTER completion. Cannot capture internal timing. Plugin subscribers may not have filesystem access. Adds plugin dependency to telemetry.

alternatives_considered.name: Middleware wrapper in SessionTools.resolve()
alternatives_considered.description: Wrap execute in session/tools.ts:108 with timing tap
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Only covers built-in tools, not MCP tools (different code path at line 153). Two injection points needed. Less granular — wraps TypedResult, not raw execution.

alternatives_considered.name: Dedicated telemetry tool
alternatives_considered.description: Create a telemetry recording tool that agents call explicitly
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Opt-in only — agents might forget. Doesn't provide systematic coverage. No latency measurement unless agent adds timing.

alternatives_considered.name: Otel-only (no JSONL)
alternatives_considered.description: Rely solely on OpenTelemetry spans for tool telemetry
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: OTel is gated by OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var. JSONL is always-on, local-first. JSONL feeds the analytics tool (analytics.ts). Both layers are needed.

risk_enumeration.risk: Performance overhead from filesystem write per invocation
risk_enumeration.mitigation: JSONL append is O(1) per line. Existing smart tools already do this (6 tools, ~same pattern). If latency is a concern, batch writes or defer to background Effect.
retry_attempt field omitted in v1. Can be added later via a ref counter in the retry layer wrapper.

risk_enumeration.risk: Cache hit detection not captured in JSONL
risk_enumeration.mitigation: OTel spans already capture cache.hit. JSONL records emit cache_hit: null with cacheable metadata. Consumers can correlate via tool_id + session_id + timestamp.

risk_enumeration.risk: Schema drift — tools change their args shape
risk_enumeration.mitigation: args_fields is a count of top-level keys, not a structural hash. Adding new optional params changes count. This is by design — provides feature-usage visibility.

risk_enumeration.risk: wrap() already has type erasure (any casts)
risk_enumeration.mitigation: tapBoth also operates on any-erased pipeline at line 208. Consistent with existing pattern. Type safety preserved at the concrete ExecuteResult level.

validation_strategy.unit_test: Create test at packages/opencode/test/tool/telemetry.test.ts — inject fake AppFileSystem, invoke a tool through wrap(), assert JSONL line written with correct fields
validation_strategy.integration_test: Run smart_grep tool, verify tool_invocations.v1.jsonl has a new line with correct tool_id='smart_grep' and latency_ms > 0
validation_strategy.analytics_test: Run analytics tool with metric='smart', verify tool counts include invocations
validation_strategy.no_regression: Existing test suite must pass — bun test from packages/opencode

explicitly_excluded: Cache hit direct detection (v2 — when getOrCompute is re-wired)
MCP tool telemetry separation (covered by same pipeline at session/tools.ts:163)
Plugin tool integration (plugin tools use tool/tool.ts:381 in registry, separate span)
deferred_to_lanes: TOOL-ERRORS: error taxonomy enrichment in telemetry
TOOL-GRAPH: cross-tool dependency for telemetry correlation
